title: Agent Core Architecture
subtitle: Agent Core Architecture and Design Patterns
description: Defines the core architectural design, lifecycle management, and state transition mechanisms of AI Agents. Covers Agent initialization, execution loops, decision processes, and resource management, forming the foundation for building reliable Agent systems.
sub_problems: Agent lifecycle management: Complete state management from initialization, execution, suspension to termination
Execution loop design: Implementation and optimization of the perception-decision-execution core cycle
Agent inheritance and composition: Design patterns for single-responsibility Agents and complex Agents
Resource lifecycle: Request, usage, and release management of memory, connections, and context
Inter-Agent communication protocol: Message passing, protocol definition, and interaction patterns between Agents
best_practices: Adopt state machine pattern to clearly define Agent states and their transition conditions
Implement clear execution loop interfaces supporting multiple execution modes: synchronous/asynchronous, streaming/batch
Reduce coupling between Agent and concrete implementations through dependency injection and interface abstraction
Provide complete initialization and cleanup hooks for Agents to ensure proper resource management
Design extensible Agent base classes supporting multiple extension approaches: inheritance, mixins, and composition
